NTC AND MOTION TO QUASH THE SUBPOENA TO MICHELLE GOMEZ, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF MARTINEZ, MAGNOLIA HEARING SET FOR APR-27-2011 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302
REMOVED FROM SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE CALENDAR SET FOR SEP-16-2011 - BY COURT.

DISCOVERY, PLAINTIFF MAGNOLIA MARTINEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH THE SUBPOENA TO MICHELLE GOMEZ, STACKS RESTAURANT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO QUASH THE SUBPOENA TO MICHELLE GOMEZ FOR PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYMENT RECORDS, SPECIFICALLY PERFORMANCE EVALUATIONS, IS DENIED. PERFORMANCE EVALUATIONS, INCLUDING CORRESPONDENCE BETWEEN SUPERVISORS AND STAFF WITH REGARD TO PERFORMANCE EVALUATIONS ARE TO BE PROVIDED. THE PERSONAL INFORMATION OF THIRD PARTIES, IF INCLUDED IN THE CORRESPONDENCE, CAN BE REDACTED (OTHER THAN THEIR NAMES). DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN THE INFORMATION REQUESTED IS DIRECTLY RELEVANT TO PLAINTIFFS DEFAMATION CLAIM AND THAT STACKS REFUSED TO PROVIDE HER WITH A LETTER OF RECOMMENDATION DUE TO THE ALLEGED DEFAMATORY REMARKS. DEFENDANT HAS ALSO SHOWN THAT THIS INFORMATION CANNOT BE PROVIDED THROUGH OTHER LESS INTRUSIVE MEANS. STACKS IS DIRECTED TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDER. MR. ROGERS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302)

NTC OF MTN AND MTN IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S MTN TO QUASH THE SUBPOENAS TO IZZY'S STEAKS & CHOPS, JACK'S RESTAURANT AND BAR AND CALIFORNIA EMPLOTMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF MARTINEZ, MAGNOLIA HEARING SET FOR AUG-12-2011 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MAGNOLIA MARTINEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH THE SUBPOENAS TO IZZY'S STEAKS & CHOPS, JACK'S RESTAURANT AND BAR AND CALIFORNIA EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH THE SUBPOENA IS GRANTED AS TO THE CALIFORNIA EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH THE SUBPOENAS AS TO IZZY'S STEAK & CHOPS AND JACK'S RESTAURANT IS DENIED. HOWEVER, THE REQUEST IS LIMITED TO RECORDS FROM THE LAST THREE YEARS. THESE ARE RELEVANT TO PLAINTIFF'S BUSINESS REPUTATION WHICH IS AT ISSUE IN THIS CASE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn In Support Of Pltf'S Mtn To Quash The Subpoenas To Izzy'S Steaks & Chops, Jack'S Restaurant And Bar And California Emplotment Development Department
Off Calendar Aug-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MAGNOLIA MARTINEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH THE SUBPOENAS TO IZZY'S STEAKS & CHOPS, JACK'S RESTAURANT AND BAR AND CALIFORNIA EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT. No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion to quash the subpoena is granted as to the California Employment Development Department. Plaintiff's motion to quash the subpoenas as to Izzy's Steak & Chops and Jack's Restaurant is denied. However, the request is limited to records from the last three years. These are relevant to plaintiff's business reputation which is at issue in this case. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported

Ntc And Motion To Quash The Subpoena To Michelle Gomez
Off Calendar Apr-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MAGNOLIA MARTINEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH THE SUBPOENA TO MICHELLE GOMEZ, STACKS RESTAURANT.
Appearances: Kate Solodky of Lawless & Lawless (391.7555) for plaintiff and moving party; Nicholas A. Rogers of Hayes Scott Bonino Ellingson & McLay, LLP (650.637.9100) for defendant Hossam Kaddoura.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: motion to quash the subpoena to Michelle Gomez for plaintiff's employment records, specifically performance evaluations, is denied. Performance evaluations, including correspondence between supervisors and staff with regard to performance evaluations are to be provided. The personal information of third parties, if included in the correspondence, can be redacted (other than their names). Defendant has shown the information requested is directly relevant to Plaintiffs defamation claim and that Stacks refused to provide her with a letter of recommendation due to the alleged defamatory remarks. Defendant has also shown that this information cannot be provided through other less intrusive means. Stacks is directed to comply with the court's order. Mr. Rogers to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-04-2011 Set For Trial On Oct-03-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Sep-16-11 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 604
